response.justification: While Bogdan brings 15+ years of software engineering experience and strong fundamentals in system design, testing, and team leadership, he is a poor fit for this C++-centric industrial automation role. His direct C++ experience is limited to 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015), with the past decade focused on JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and web-based systems. He lacks any background in IoT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 104), industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), or embedded/real-time systems—all core to the role.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance systems thinking from Dunnhumby microservices work handling millions of requests/second with low-latency requirements
Strong mentorship and software quality culture: organized refactoring dojos, created testing workshops, and consistently applied TDD across roles
Full-stack architectural capability with exposure to distributed systems, event-driven patterns, and REST APIs across multiple domains
concerns: C++ expertise gap: only 6 months of direct C++ experience in 2014–2015; past 10 years entirely in JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js and web technologies
No industrial automation or IoT background; no experience with industrial protocols, embedded systems, or on-premises automation machinery
Missing security and compliance credentials: no evidence of SDL practices, IEC 62443 implementation, or industrial cybersecurity standards knowledge
